description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, dark metallic blade featuring two symmetrical cutting edges and a central spike. The blade is mounted on a long, cylindrical handle that appears to be made of wood with metallic reinforcements at both ends. The handle is straight and features a segmented grip area, likely for improved handling and control. The blade design is aggressive, with angular cutouts and a broad profile, suggesting it is intended for heavy-duty use. The overall construction combines both functional and decorative elements, with a visually striking contrast between the dark blade and the lighter handle. The axe is of a size and style that would make it suitable for display, ceremonial use, or as a prop in urban-themed environments.
description: A double-headed battle axe with a dark, angular blade and a long, reinforced wooden handle. The design is both functional and decorative, with a striking contrast between the blade and handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the symmetrical double-headed blade and central spike, with the handle extending downward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the full length of the straight handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the double-edged blade and handle alignment.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the blade's thinness and the handle's length.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood with metallic and leather grip segments, no visible curvature, ergonomic grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical, angular blade with two cutting edges and a central spike, broad profile with decorative cutouts.
attribute_mounting_style: Modern reinforced mounting with visible metallic collars at the blade-handle junction.
